flavor: The area around the new Shadowlands would yield no crops, and none would eat them if it did.
"Sometimes the earth remembers, and sometimes there are wounds that cannot be healed." - Isawa Emori

text: Absent Battle, :bow:: If you are the Defender, move your target performing Personality to the current battlefield. If this moved him and he is now opposed, straighten him and gain 2 Honor.
title: Blighted Region

text: Open, :bow:, :gstar:: Target your performing Personality to Equip a target non-Unique Follower or Item in your discard pile to him.
title: Border Market

flavor: Once green and lush, the God-Beast left the lands scarred and wounded.

text:
After this card enters play: Lose 4 Honor.
Limited: If there are any cards in this province, discard this card: Discard a card from the province, refilling it face-up with an Undead Personality in your discard pile.
title: Deadlands

flavor: Rebuilding, one stone at a time.
The Crab want for jade, but never steel.

text:
Reaction, :g2:: After your performing Personality enters play from a Province, create a +1F/+1C Weapon Item and attach it to him.
Reaction, :g2:: After your performing Personality enters play from a province, create a +1F Armor Item and attach it to him.
title: Kaiu Village

flavor: Even in the new kingdom, there are places that the samurai choose not to acknowledge.
designernotes: [Note: This is a Market inside the "Safe Zone" in the colonies.]

text: Open: Bow this card, choose your performing Personality and target an attachment in your hand: Attach it to him, paying 2 less Gold, to a minimum of 1.
title: Low Market

flavor: As the Empire grew, new areas became the subject of conflict between the Great Clans. Although it took great effort to accurately survey new lands, those who did enjoyed great benefits during times of conflict.

text: Battle: Discard the top 3 cards of your Fate deck and target an attacking Personality: Move him home.
title: Overgrown Grove

mrp: If this card is destroyed, the extra Dynasty card no longer automatically goes away (though the next time the province refills, it only holds one card like normal).
This card no longer reveals one of its two Dynasty cards the turn it comes into play

flavor: Once the seat of power for an Empire, it became overgrown and neglected over the course of years.
"Throne of our ancestors; crossroads of the future. My heart lightens when I see your walls across the fields." - Toturi Tsudao
"Amazing that such beauty can be found so close to the fallen city." - Ikoma Otemi

text:
This province holds two Dynasty cards; do not reveal the second card in the Events Phase this card enters play. You decide each time which card slot is revealed, resolved, or refilled first.
Before this province is destroyed: Lose 10 Honor.
title: Plains of Otosan Uchi

flavor: Swamps are home to many threats: disease, bogs, and predators…

text: Reaction: After the resolution of an action that bowed a Personality at a battlefield at this province or an adjacent province, target him and bow this card: Negate his next straightening (this turn).
title: Quagmire

flavor: "The sisters abide there still. Are they the original sisters, or newcomers? Does it matter?" - Togashi Osawa
"The Dark Prophet conspires to destroy the wisdom Rokugan has earned."

text: Limited: Destroy this card and remove your performing unbowed Personality from the game: Search your Fate deck for a card with a lower Focus Value than your Personality's Chi. Show it and put it in your hand.
title: Refuge of the Three Sisters

flavor: "I am sorry to interrupt you, but your services are required."

text: Limited: If it is not your first or second turn: Bring a face-up Personality in this province into play, paying all costs. You may ignore the Personality's Honor Requirement if he is Lion Clan.
title: Shinden Shorai

flavor: Chosai left little unharmed.

text:
Defending Earth Personalities have +1F while at this province's battlefield.
Battle: Even if you control no units at the current battlefield, bow your performing Earth Personality at any location: Give the current battlefield's province +3 strength.
title: Stone-Hewed Shrine

flavor: "Rokugan must stand together to survive. How often must we learn this simple lesson?" - Asako Bare

text: Open: If it is not your turn, choose your two unbowed performing Personalities with Clan alignments that do not share a Clan alignment, and bow one of them: Gain 1 Honor. Give your provinces +2 strength.
title: Temple of the Seven Fortunes

flavor: Some called it the Temple of the Builders, as there was always a shrine under construction.

text:
Before you lose Honor, destroy this Region to negate the Honor loss.
Limited, :bow:: If your Family Honor is 9 or lower, gain 1 Honor.
title: Ten Thousand Temples

flavor:
The light places had fallen to darkness.
The dark places had become something much worse.

text: Open, :bow:: Bow one or two target attachments.
title: The Distant Reaches

flavor: "Most of the coast is in ruins. We will not disturb it, other than to harvest what resources remain within. The gods frown upon the wasteful." - Yoritomo Naizen

text: Battle: Choose your performing Personality and bow this card: Move him home. Straighten his unit if he moved.
notes: Released in the 2011 Summer Con pack
title: The Ivory Coast

flavor: A shining jewel amid the wilderness.

text:
Each of your cards that is Courtier or Magistrate has +1F.
Political Open, :bow:: Target a face-down card in a Province. You claim a new tract of land. Turn the face-down card face-up. You may increase the Province's strength by 2.
title: The Second City

text:
Your Nonhuman Personalities enter play from this Province for 1 less Gold.
Reaction, :bow:: Before an action resolves, negate its effects that move any Personalities into an attacking army at this Province's battlefield.
notes: Released to the 2012 Level 1 GST participants
title: Verdant Jungle

notes: Released to the Before the Dawn pre-release participants

flavor: There were those that thought that Rokugan would never be the same after this war. Where the dead fell, the Lion hard to burn, leaving nothing but destruction behind.

text: Limited: Discard this Region to destroy a target Region.
title: War-ravaged Plain

flavor: "The kami here are strong. I do not know what caused the devastation, but I believe the land can be renewed." - Agasha Kurou
text:
This province has +2 strength.
Reaction: After a Melee or Ranged Attack is targeted: Give it -2 strength.
designernotes: The long, treacherous overland route to the Ivory Kingdoms

text:
This province has +2 strength.
Reaction: After a Melee or Ranged Attack is targeted: Give it -2 strength.
title: Fallow Lands

flavor: "Magnificent, are they not? I would not see their home despoiled by conflict." - Kitsune Kichi
text: Limited: Pay 2 Gold and target your Personality: Create a 1F Nonhuman • Falcon • Cavalry Follower and attach it to him.
designernotes: [Art Note: A section of jungle in the Ivory Kingdoms which is being used as a Falconry.]

text: Limited, :g2:: Create a 1F Nonhuman • Falcon • Cavalry Follower and attach it to your target Personality.
title: Natural Aviary

flavor: "It is called such because, allegedly, there is always something… interesting… happening there. The definition of interesting varies, of course." - Miya Kitori
text: Reaction: After a Personality enters play from this province: Refill the province face-up.
designernotes: [Art Note: A place where it is easy for Personalities to prep for war, allowing the province to be more productive.]

text: Reaction: After a Personality enters play from this Province, refill the Province face-up.
notes: Released to the 2012 Quarter 4 Organized Play participants
title: Interesting Plains

notes: Released in the 2012 Summer Con pack

flavor: The domestication of the Colonies proceeded slowly for many years, and for good reason. The rapid expansion authorized by the new governor came with a number of deadly perils that claimed many lives.
text: Battle: Destroy this card and target a Follower or Personality without Followers at this province's battlefield: Destroy it.
Battle: Destroy this card and target a Follower or Personality without Followers at this province's battlefield: Destroy it.
title: Quicksand

flavor: "These lands were not like this the last time I came through. It is as if they have been cursed by all Seven Fortunes, and not even mold will grow now." - From the last, unsubmitted report of a missing Tsuruchi scout
designernotes: [Art Note: This Region represents the bad land your opponent has been forced to build his fortifications on, making them weaker. The land is weak enough to even affect adjacent lands, though admittedly for a lesser effect]

text:
After this Region enters play, attach it to another player's Province (that can attach a Region; that player now controls it.)
After a Holding is revealed in this Province, discard it and this Region.
title: Tested Land

flavor: "These bandits have preyed upon too many caravans already. Spread word of a new caravan, one loaded with riches bound for the Empire. When they throw open the carts, the bandits will find only justice awaiting them within." - Shinjo Tselu
designernotes: Art Note: A secret pass that only Bandits know about which allows them to quickly and safely retreat if necessary.

text: Your Ronin have +1F.
notes: Released in the special Imperial Assembly pack in May 2013
title: Ambush Pass

flavor: "I do not understand. A year ago this land was dead." - Matsu Koyama
designernotes: [Note: These are the Lion Lands that the Spider nuked and are now more fertile.]

text: After a Farm Holding enters play from this province: Give it a +1 Gold Production token and refill this province face-up.
title: Renewed Lands

flavor: "The lands approaching the Second City have been fortified by the local lord, Mirumoto Kokure. It seems he too has embraced the corrupting influences of this foreign land."
use: Defensive Region outside the walls of the Second City, all prepped and ready for the oncoming legions
designernotes: [Note: This is the region outside of the Second City walls, fortified up and ready to fight off the incoming army. Because it is being used for defenses, it no longer is producing resources, but it is giving you extra cards]

text:
This Province does not hold Dynasty cards.
Limited, :bow:: Draw a card.
title: Kokure's Lands
